programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: Kate's mother pressures her to cut the leash on her dog; Frankie misreads Anne's advice; Anne's daughter starts to bond with the nanny.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: Kate is shamed for her adult movie preference; Jenny makes a move with Marvin; Frankie searches for the root of her intimacy issues.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: Kate makes a discovery at work; Anne tries to bond with her daughter; Jenny helps Ian with his movie.

programme.title: Workin' Moms
programme.desc: Eleanor tries to help Kate find a new nanny; Jenny organizes a bachelorette party; Frankie and Giselle get some help with their intimacy issue.

programme.title: Strays
programme.desc: Shannon enlists her new neighbor, a reporter, to do a story on the shelter; Paul reluctantly agrees to give Kristian a tattoo.

programme.title: Baroness von Sketch Show
programme.sub-title: There's A Special Place in Hell
programme.desc: No one wants to be a gay cliche; after 40, you're gonna want to exclusively drink white wine; a fierce manicure.

programme.title: CBC Winnipeg Comedy Festival
programme.desc: Howie Miller hosts a gala night of comedy about insecurities, bad habits and personal shortcomings; featuring Julie Kim, Matt Wright, and Kathleen McGee.

programme.title: Comedy Night With Rick Mercer
programme.desc: Canadian comics Sophie Buddle, Kyle Brownrigg, Dave Merheje and Jean Paul.

programme.title: Comedy Night With Rick Mercer
programme.desc: Featuring performances by DeAnne Smith, Keith Pedro, Seán Cullen and Alex Wood.

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: Mark and Ritchie receive police protection after witnessing a grizzly crime on Halloween night; Fox grows tired of her brother's increasingly delinquent behavior.

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: Mark is considering what life would be like as a priest; as rumors of scandal amongst the clergy start to mount, Mark reassesses what his religion means to him.

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: Mark collects Christmas toys for the needy, which leads to an uncomfortable discovery about one of his classmates; Pop's prodigal son shows up.

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: Mark and Mike bond over Mark's role in a school play; Pop turns to Mary for help after the CRA learns he's been collecting his dead wife's pension checks.

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: The Critch men are left to their own devices after Mary is hospitalized for a minor procedure; Mark has trouble balancing the household chores and accidentally gives Pops the wrong pills.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience
programme.desc: A new church member makes Umma feel cast aside; Appa becomes jealous of Janet's journaling; Shannon's decision to allow shorts in the office allows Terence to come out of his shell.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience
programme.desc: Appa destroys a piece of Gerald's childhood; Umma misrepresents herself to her support group; Shannon gets in the way of Kimchee's plan to visit Jung in California.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience
programme.desc: Umma and Appa try to fit in at an upscale tennis court; Shannon helps Omar deal with his over-eager family; Janet misjudges a co-worker.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience
programme.desc: Appa's nerves are tested when Nayoung arrives with a furry friend; Umma shares an uncomfortable lunch with Jung and Shannon; bad timing makes Kimchee neglect a co-worker in need.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience
programme.desc: Appa finds marijuana in the house; Shannon throws a staff party that doesn't go as planned.

programme.title: Kim's Convenience
programme.desc: Appa and Kimchee enjoy an impromptu hangout together; Janet and Umma find an envelope of money in the store; Jung and Shannon struggle to watch TV together.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry comes up with a scheme to catch a basketball game on a school trip to Boston; his plan might fall apart due to Robert coming along as a chaperone.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry develops a crush on an annoying student; Simon gets driving lessons; Trudy and Robert become incomprehensible.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry steps in when Bobbi and Craig break up; Robert disciplines students; Jimmy asks advice on growing wheatgrass.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Gerry's date with Jean McGrory goes well until a gorgeous sex worker bursts in demanding her due; Leung bangs his head against a door and the students post the video online; Trudy regrets her decision.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: Sean and Miranda's relationship is on the rocks, and Gerry feels he has to meddle; Robert wants to punish a student; Simon agrees to donate blood.

programme.title: Mr. D
programme.desc: When Gerry runs into a distraught Daniel Negreanu, he organizes a staff poker night to help him; things seem to be going well until Wayne Leung ends up winning.

programme.title: Son of a Critch
programme.desc: Mark and Ritchie receive police protection after witnessing a grizzly crime on Halloween night; Fox grows tired of her brother's increasingly delinquent behavior.
